channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: O aumento do número de turistas europeus no Brasil
item.description: Brazil recorded 411,804 European tourist arrivals in the first two months of 2026, an 18.55% increase compared to the same period in 2025, driven by significant growth from Portugal (+29.7%), Germany (+17%), the UK (+14.5%) and France (+8.6%). In 2025, the country welcomed 1.77 million European tourists, a 19.54% rise from 2024, following a 20% increase between 2023 and 2024. Embratur president Marcelo Freixo is stepping down from his post this Wednesday to run for federal deputy from Rio de Janeiro.

item.title: A disputa entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro em MG, CE, SC e AP, segundo a AtlasIntel
item.description: AtlasIntel poll reveals a polarized race between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in four Brazilian states. Lula leads comfortably in Ceará, while Flávio Bolsonaro leads in Santa Catarina and Amapá. In Minas Gerais, Lula holds a technical lead, but the race remains within the margin of error, indicating a tight contest.

item.title: EUA mentiram para atacar o Irã, afirma Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva accused the United States of lying about Iran’s nuclear program to justify war against the country, stating the conflict is raising fuel prices and pressuring Brazil’s economy. He referenced the 2010 Tehran Declaration, brokered by Brazil, Turkey, and Iran, which allowed Iran to enrich uranium for peaceful purposes under international monitoring — an agreement rejected by the U.S. and the European Union. Lula said the Brazilian government is implementing fuel tax limits and active monitoring to prevent price abuses and shield vulnerable populations from the war’s economic impact.

item.title: Crusoé: Dr. Furlan lidera corrida ao governo no Amapá, aponta AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on April 1 shows that former Macapá mayor Antônio Furlan, known as Dr. Furlan (PSD), leads the race for governor of Amapá with 65.7% of voting intentions in the first round. Incumbent governor and re-election candidate Clécio Luís (União Brasil) follows with 31%. Other candidates were not reported with significant percentages.

item.title: O desmonte do espólio de Cláudio Castro pelo desembargador Ricardo Couto
item.description: Acting Rio Governor Ricardo Couto has dismissed several allies of Cláudio Castro, including Gutemberg Fonseca, former Consumer Defense Secretary appointed by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, who is expected to run for federal deputy under the PL. Also removed were Jair Bittencourt, former Government Secretary, and Edu Guimarães from the Institutional Security Cabinet, amid a judicial imbroglio extending Couto’s tenure. The moves signal a dismantling of Castro’s political legacy in the Guanabara Palace.

item.title: AP: Pesquisa aponta vitória de Dr. Furlan no 1º turno para o governo
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on Wednesday, April 1st, shows Macapá Mayor Dr. Furlan (PSD) would win the Amapá gubernatorial election in the first round with 65.7% of voting intentions, against 31% for incumbent Governor Clécio Luis (União Brasil). In a simulated runoff, Furlan leads 65.4% to 31.7%. The survey highlights Furlan’s strong positive image (75%) and Luis’s high disapproval (59%), with crime, corruption, and healthcare identified as top voter concerns.

item.title: Pesquisa AtlasIntel reforça o favoritismo do casal Furlan nas eleições no Amapá
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows Dr. Furlan (PSD) leading the Amapá gubernatorial race with 67.7% in Scenario 1 and 65.4% in Scenario 2, ahead of incumbent governor Clécio Luís (União Brasil). His wife, Rayssa Furlan (Podemos), leads the Senate race with 33.7% to 34.4%, followed by Lucas Barreto (PSD). Dr. Furlan’s recent resignation following a STF investigation did not significantly damage his popularity.

item.title: Lira dá ultimato a prefeito de Maceió e ameaça se aliar a ex-relator da CPI
item.description: Former Chamber of Deputies president Arthur Lira (PP-AL), a pre-candidate for the Senate, issued an ultimatum to Maceio’s mayor JHC to commit to joining his 2026 election ticket. Lira threatened to ally with the former rapporteur of the Public Works CPI if the mayor refuses, signaling a potential breakdown in local political alignment. The move highlights growing tensions within Alagoas’ governing coalition ahead of the state and national elections.

item.title: Eleição direta no RJ pode virar campanha para Flávio Bolsonaro e preocupa aliados de Paes
item.description: Allies of former Rio mayor Eduardo Paes fear that a special direct election for governor, scheduled for June, could become a campaign opportunity for Senator Flávio Bolsonaro to bolster his political standing and support for PL candidate Douglas Ruas, with an eye toward a future presidential run. Despite Paes leading in polls (46% vs. 13% for Ruas), allies worry Flávio must travel across the state to consolidate Bolsonaro family influence, relying on estimated support from 70 of Rio’s 92 mayors. The STF will rule on the legality of the direct election on April 8.

item.title: Sem maioria no Senado, Lula diz que “senador pensa que é Deus”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that senators, feeling 'like God' due to their eight-year mandates, can create problems for the government if there is no solid base in the Senate. He emphasized the importance of building an allied base in Congress, including allowing ministers to leave office to run for Senate seats, as a central strategy for 2026. Lula also stressed the need to negotiate with allies beyond the PT, citing Camilo Santana as an example of effective governance through political dialogue and alliances.

item.title: STF julga se Jair Bolsonaro podia bloquear cidadãos em contas oficiais nas redes sociais
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) will rule on May 20 on two cases examining whether former President Jair Bolsonaro could block citizens on his official social media accounts (Twitter/X and Instagram) while in office, allegedly violating freedom of expression and access to information. The plaintiffs — journalist William de Luca Martinez and lawyer Leonardo Medeiros Magalhães — argue the blocks constituted censorship and abuse of power, while Bolsonaro’s defense maintained his accounts were private and that he had the right to choose his followers, like any private citizen.

item.title: Fala de Lula sobre senadores irrita líderes e tensiona ambiente para análise de indicação de Messias
item.description: President Lula's remark that senators "think they are God" provoked backlash in Congress, particularly from the opposition, who viewed it as an attempt to subordinate the legislature and a threat to Jorge Messias' STF nomination. While centrist senators downplayed public impact, there are private concerns that the comment undermines government coalition-building and could chill negotiations over the nomination, especially among undecided members of the CCJ.

item.title: Os destaques da pauta de abril e maio no STF: Reforma tributária, eleições no RJ e Ferrogrão
item.description: The STF's agenda for April and May 2026 features high-impact electoral and economic cases, including the decision on whether the Rio de Janeiro interim government election should be direct (popular vote) or indirect (legislative vote), the constitutionality of the law reducing Jamanxim National Park to enable the Ferrogrão railway, and the payroll tax exemption for 17 sectors. Additional topics include tax reform, the appointment of relatives to public offices (already supported by a majority), petroleum royalty distribution rules, and lawsuits on gender pay equity and access to financial intelligence reports without judicial authorization.

item.title: Ibovespa avança com alívio global e otimismo sobre possível trégua no Oriente Médio
item.description: The Ibovespa rose on Wednesday, tracking global optimism over a possible ceasefire in the Middle East following statements by Donald Trump suggesting the conflict could end soon. Major Brazilian banks traded higher, with Banco do Brasil leading gains. Domestically, President Lula attended events in Ceará, including the inauguration of ITA campus works and the two-year anniversary of the Pé-de-Meia program, before traveling to Salvador.

item.title: Nova lei obriga municípios a oferecer vagas em creches e pré-escolas também em áreas rurais
item.description: The new Law 15.369/26 requires municipalities to offer vacancies in daycare centers and preschools in both urban and rural areas, proportionally to each zone's population. The amendment to the National Education Guidelines and Framework Law (LDB) aims to reduce inequality in access to early childhood education between cities and countryside. The measure was signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and originated from Bill 8618/17 by Deputy Damião Feliciano (União-PB).

item.title: Sancionado o aumento gradual da licença-paternidade
item.description: President Lula signed Law 15.371, gradually increasing paternity leave from 5 to 20 days between 2027 and 2029, ensuring full pay, job stability, and provisions for adoption and vulnerable families. The law also establishes paternity salary as full pay proportional to leave duration, with reimbursement possible for employers, especially micro and small businesses.

item.title: Governo Lula estuda regulamentação dos artigos 19 e 21 do Marco Civil da Internet
item.description: The Lula government is studying regulation of Articles 19 and 21 of the Marco Civil da Internet to clarify platform liability, particularly regarding online misogyny. The initiative builds on a recent STF ruling on Article 19 but avoids including points challenged via embargos. The goal is to harmonize interpretations among the judiciary, agencies, and platforms, focusing on notification, action, and duty of care obligations, though publication of the decrees remains uncertain.

item.title: Lula arrebentou com os pobres; pela terceira vez
item.description: The author claims that Lula has harmed the poor on three occasions, citing articles written in 2016 during Dilma Rousseff's recession and again in 2026. He criticizes the fiscal management of Lula's first two administrations, arguing they left a pre-famine situation and explosive debt, which Dilma inherited. The text suggests a recurring pattern of harm to the vulnerable under Lula's leadership.

item.title: Lei amplia licença-paternidade para 20 dias e cria salário-paternidade
item.description: President Lula signed Law 15.371/26, which gradually extends paternity leave from 5 to 20 days — 10 days starting in 2027, 15 in 2028, and 20 in 2029. The law also creates a paternity salary benefit, providing income during leave for formal and informal workers, including MEIs, domestic workers, and special insured persons. It strengthens shared parental responsibility, equates paternity leave with maternity leave as a social right, ensures job stability, and extends benefits to adoptive parents and in cases of child disability or maternal absence.

item.title: Lula sobre alta no diesel: “Vamos ter que colocar alguém na cadeia”
item.description: President Lula stated the government will hold accountable and punish those responsible for abusive increases in diesel prices, saying they will 'put someone in jail'. He linked the price hikes to the Iran-U.S.-Israel conflict, noting Brazil imports about 30% of its diesel. Measures include zeroing federal taxes, a R$ 0.32 per liter subsidy, and a R$ 30 billion package, contrasting with Bolsonaro's temporary tax cuts.

item.title: Alcolumbre não queria que Lula formalizasse Messias ao STF?
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre directly asked President Lula not to formalize the nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court at this time, according to Metrópoles. The request was made during an in-person meeting between the two on Sunday, April 29. Despite the appeal, Lula announced Messias's nomination to the STF.

item.title: Lula chama Ciro de “destemperado”, mas diz que foi um bom ministro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) labeled former minister Ciro Gomes (PSDB) as "temperamental," saying he sometimes speaks before thinking and creates unnecessary conflicts, while acknowledging Ciro was a good minister during his tenure (2003-2006). Lula contrasted Ciro’s party-hopping with his own lifelong loyalty to the PT and suggested Ciro’s criticisms stem from his presidential ambitions. Despite tensions, Lula said he prefers to remember the positive moments and will not interfere in PT strategies in Ceará, expressing confidence in Governor Elmano de Freitas’ re-election, even as Ciro leads in local polls.

item.title: Lula defende ‘colocar alguém na cadeia’ por alta dos combustíveis
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended the possibility of imprisoning those raising fuel prices, even after the government's tax exemptions. He accused bad-faith actors of deliberately increasing prices and announced that the Federal Police and state Procons are conducting investigations to punish those responsible.

item.title: Lula diz que é mentira que o Irã tem bomba nuclear e chama guerra de Trump de desnecessária
item.description: Lula stated that it is false that Iran possesses nuclear weapons and called the war by the United States and Israel against Iran unnecessary, referencing a 2010 agreement he helped negotiate to allow peaceful uranium enrichment. He criticized the war’s impact on Brazil’s economy, particularly on food and fuel prices, and pledged to act to prevent the conflict from raising the cost of essential goods like beans, lettuce, and meat.

item.title: Eduardo Leite rechaça convite do PSDB e diz que fica no PSD mesmo após se queixar de escolha de Caiado para o Planalto
item.description: Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) has decided to stay in his party and not run for any office in the 2026 elections, despite criticizing the PSD’s nomination of Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate and rejecting invitations to rejoin the PSDB or lead a manifesto against polarization. He will complete his term as governor of Rio Grande do Sul, amid internal challenges as the PL’s Zucco gains strength and key allies like União Brasil-PP and Republicanos shift support away from his preferred candidate, Gabriel Souza (MDB).

item.title: Cade pode ter até 3 cadeiras vazias ainda no primeiro semestre de 2026
item.description: The vacancy in the presidency of CADE since July 2025, combined with the end of the terms of Councilors Gustavo Augusto and Victor Fernandes, may leave the body operating with a minimum quorum (four of seven members) in the first half of 2026. The difficulty in appointing new members by President Lula is expected to intensify as the 2026 electoral period approaches, delaying nominations and approvals. If new councilors are not named and approved this year, the increased workload per remaining member could prolong the processing time of cases before the Tribunal.

item.title: A preocupação de Lula com as eleições para o Senado
item.description: Lula emphasized the importance of Senate elections for future governability, warning that senators with long terms can become uncontrollable without a sustaining base. He praised Ceará Governor Elmano Freitas, avoided direct confrontation with Ciro Gomes, and criticized the PT for its hegemony in the state, advocating that alliance partners should also nominate candidates for the Senate slate.

item.title: A nova declaração de Silvio Almeida sobre acusação de importunação sexual
item.description: Former Human Rights Minister Silvio Almeida broke his months-long silence to assert his innocence regarding sexual harassment allegations against Minister Anielle Franco. He stated he remained silent out of respect for the family’s pain and will defend himself in court, condemning his removal from office as violent and unjust, and linking it to racial stereotypes that portray Black men as inherently aggressive or uncontrollable. The PGR’s complaint to the STF remains under seal, and Almeida was indicted by the Federal Police in November 2025 for the same offense.

item.title: Senador com mandato de 8 anos pensa que é Deus, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) stated that a senator with an eight-year term 'thinks he is God' and could create problems for the Planalto Palace if the government lacks support in Congress. The remark was made during a press briefing on Wednesday (January 1st) and highlights growing tensions in the government's congressional outreach.

item.title: Lula acredita que ambiente no Senado mudou e está favorável à aprovação de Messias ao STF
item.description: President Lula believes the Senate environment has become favorable for approving Jorge Messias as his fifth nominee to the Supreme Federal Court (STF), despite lacking active support from Davi Alcolumbre. The Senate president has assured the government he will not oppose the nomination, which is seen as vital for Messias’ confirmation. The Senate’s Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) is expected to hold Messias’ confirmation hearing between late April and early May.

item.title: Flávio tem 53,4% e venceria no 1º turno em SC, diz pesquisa
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on April 1, 2026, shows Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) leading with 53.4% of voting intentions in Santa Catarina, enough to win the first round of the 2026 presidential election. Former President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) trails at 28.4%. In simulated runoff scenarios, either Flávio or Jair Bolsonaro defeat Lula by margins exceeding 59%.

item.title: Alerj estuda data de nova eleição e prepara alternativas jurídicas para depois do julgamento do STF
item.description: The Alerj is discussing a new date for the election of its presidency and preparing legal strategies ahead of the STF ruling on April 8 regarding the interim mandate. The Alerj's Prosecutor's Office is drafting a resource to advance the election before the TRE's vote recount homologation on April 14, challenging the competency and procedure of the injunction that annulled Douglas Ruas's election. Around 40 deputies who supported Ruas met with the legal team to map post-ruling pathways.

item.title: Lula diz que há ‘gente mau caráter’ elevando preço do diesel e fala em punição
item.description: President Lula stated that market agents are improperly raising diesel prices despite government measures like the exemption of PIS and Cofins taxes, and announced intensified oversight by the Federal Police and consumer protection agencies, including potential arrests.

item.title: Camilo Santana é um líder e cabo eleitoral importante, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula praised Education Minister Camilo Santana as a national leader and confirmed his departure from the ministry to serve as a key electoral operative for the PT in the 2026 elections, aiming to strengthen the party’s presence in the Northeast and nationwide. Lula stated that Santana will gain greater political freedom by returning to the Senate and should be deployed nationally to boost his profile and build new party leaders, while expressing preference that he not run for office in Ceará, commending current governor Elmano de Freitas.

item.title: Endividamento das famílias se aproxima da máxima histórica, diz BC
item.description: Household debt in Brazil reached 49.7% of disposable income in January 2026, close to the historical high of 49.9% recorded in July 2022, according to Central Bank data. Excluding housing credit, the indicator drops to 31.3%, indicating that approximately 40% of total household debt is tied to mortgage financing. The Lula government is studying measures to reduce household debt amid concerns over elevated income commitment levels.

item.title: Nunes Marques suspende afastamento de Rodrigo Manga, o prefeito ‘tiktoker’ de Sorocaba
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Nunes Marques granted a preliminary injunction suspending the removal of Rodrigo Manga, the 'TikTok mayor' of Sorocaba, who had been barred from office for 145 days amid a federal police investigation into alleged misappropriation of public health funds. The ruling emphasized that the contested contract was signed by SAAE, an indirect municipal agency, and thus does not directly implicate the mayor. The decision remains provisional and must be ratified by the STF’s Second Chamber.

item.title: Charge do JCaesar: 1º de abril
item.description: The JCaesar cartoon published on April 1st, 2026, is a political satire targeting the Lula government, using April Fool's Day as a framework to mock or question official narratives. Published in VEJA, the cartoon features President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva as the central figure, employing the artist’s signature humorous style to comment on Brazil’s current political climate. While it does not specify particular policies, the piece contributes to public discourse through irony and caricature.

item.title: Lula diz que medidas adotadas para conter preços dos combustíveis não têm 'nada a ver' com as tomadas no governo Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that the measures adopted by his government to contain fuel prices have nothing to do with those taken during Jair Bolsonaro's administration. The comments were made during an interview with Grupo Cidade de Comunicação in Ceará on Wednesday, April 1st. The report is currently being updated.

item.title: Exército oficializa Cláudia Gusmão como primeira mulher general no Brasil; saiba quem é
item.description: The Brazilian Army officially appointed Dr. Cláudia Lima Gusmão Cacho as the country's first female general, following a promotion signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. The ceremony took place at the Army Club in Brasília, where she received the sword and command baton, symbols of her rank. Her career represents a historic milestone in the inclusion of women in the Armed Forces, which only began admitting them into permanent ranks in the 1990s.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Pix volta à pauta dos EUA e pode afetar cenário eleitoral
item.description: The United States may renew pressure on Brazil's Pix payment system within the Section 301 investigation, according to sources cited by JOTA PRO Poder, aiming to gain leverage in bilateral relations. The issue is seen as a potential risk of electoral interference by the Trump administration, given Pix’s broad national popularity. Meanwhile, Lula has formally nominated Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court, awaiting only the release of the presidential message by Senator Davi Alcolumbre to proceed with the confirmation hearing in the Senate.

item.title: Nunes Marques suspende afastamento de Rodrigo Manga da prefeitura de Sorocaba (SP)
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Kassio Nunes Marques suspended the removal of Sorocaba Mayor Rodrigo Manga, who had been temporarily removed by the TRF-3 and STJ over alleged corruption in health contracts under Operation Copia e Cola. The ruling found the removal an excessive intervention and a threat to Manga’s electoral rights in the 2026 race, with the candidacy deadline set for April 4. Manga may now return to office or step down to run for office after nearly five months of absence.

item.title: O pronunciamento de Silvio Almeida, denunciado por importunação sexual contra Anielle: ‘Acusações irresponsáveis’
item.description: Silvio Almeida, former Minister of Human Rights, publicly denied allegations of sexual harassment against Minister Anielle Franco, calling the accusations 'irresponsible' and claiming they were politically motivated to remove him from public life. He argued he was unable to defend himself during the investigation and linked the case to broader stereotypes against Black men. The PGR’s complaint, based on Franco’s testimony, is under seal at the STF with Justice André Mendonça as rapporteur.

item.title: Licença-paternidade: especialista explica como a lei pode ajudar na inserção das mulheres no mercado de trabalho
item.description: The expansion of paternity leave, approved by Congress and signed by President Lula, is an important step toward reducing gender inequality in the labor market, according to Cecilia Machado, chief economist at BoCOM BBM and professor at PUC-Rio. The policy aims to encourage shared parental responsibilities, enabling women to maintain or return to work after childbirth.

item.title: Cleitinho lidera disputa pelo governo de MG, diz AtlasIntel
item.description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo leads voting intentions for Minas Gerais governor with 32.7% against Rodrigo Pacheco's 28.6% in a scenario including other candidates, according to an AtlasIntel poll released on April 1, 2026. In a projected runoff, Cleitinho defeats Pacheco 47% to 42%. The survey, registered with the TSE, interviewed 2,195 people from March 25–30, 2026, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Milei reduz a pobreza, mas é o presidente mais impopular
item.description: Argentina recorded a poverty reduction from 38.1% in 2024 to 28.2% in 2025, with 13.2 million people in poverty according to Indec, attributed to Javier Milei's liberal economic program. Despite this success, Milei remains the most unpopular president in Latin America, with 62% disapproval and 36% support, per AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll. His approval is comparable to Peru's interim president José Balcázar (61% disapproval), while Lula and Gustavo Petro also show high rejection rates.

item.title: Ciro Gomes lidera disputa pelo governo do Ceará, diz Paraná Pesquisas
item.description: Ciro Gomes leads the race for governor of Ceará with 46.6% voting intentions, according to Paraná Pesquisas, ahead of Elmano de Freitas (33.9%). In a potential runoff, Ciro would win with 53.3% against Elmano’s 36.4%. Captain Wagner (União Brasil) is the frontrunner for the Senate, and Governor Elmano’s administration is approved by 54.1% of voters.

item.title: Após silêncio, Silvio Almeida diz ser inocente e vítima de racismo
item.description: Silvio Almeida, former Minister of Human Rights, declared himself innocent of sexual harassment allegations in a video, claiming he is a victim of racism and a public lynching orchestrated by political adversaries. He said he remained silent out of respect for his family, the law, and the secrecy of the investigation, and will only defend himself in court. Almeida was indicted by the Federal Police in November 2025, charged by the Attorney General’s Office in March 2026, and dismissed from Lula’s government in September 2024.

item.title: Fernando Morais reúne convidados no lançamento de livro sobre Lula
item.description: Writer, biographer, and journalist Fernando Morais launched the second volume of his biographical trilogy on Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, titled 'Lula, Volume 2', at an event held on Monday, December 30, in Rio de Janeiro. Published by Companhia das Letras, the book covers Lula’s political and personal trajectory during his early presidencies and rise to power.

item.title: Silvio Almeida quebra silêncio após ser denunciado por importunação sexual contra Anielle Franco: 'Inocente'
item.description: Silvio Almeida, former Minister of Human Rights in Lula's government, denied the sexual harassment accusations against Anielle Franco and declared himself innocent, criticizing his dismissal and suggesting political motives behind the PGR's denunciation.

item.title: Trump fará pronunciamento à nação hoje à noite
item.description: Donald Trump will deliver a national address on Wednesday at 9 p.m. (10 p.m. in Brazil) to provide an important update on Iran, following his recent statement that a deal is 'irrelevant' and that Iran will not be able to develop nuclear weapons 'for a good while.' The announcement, made by White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt, has created suspense, as Trump typically speaks off-the-cuff at any time; the scheduled timing suggests a possible political motive, such as bolstering his narrative ahead of the 2026 midterm elections or reinforcing his foreign policy stance.
